- deep-research: Deep research harness вЂ” fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly вЂ” if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highв†’max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only вЂ” it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK вЂ” model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER вЂ” read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" вЂ” whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) вЂ” never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named вЂ” don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

164	def _registered_slug_for_task(tid: str) -> str:
165	    """Resolve the DRB task id to a stable, human-readable run-dir slug.
166	
167	    Since the fresh entry is now ``run_one_query`` DIRECT (NOT ``run_gate_b_query``), the slug
168	    NO LONGER drives any DRB-II canonical-question binding вЂ” run_one_query keys everything on
169	    ``q["question"]`` (the verbatim DRB-v1 prompt) and uses ``q["slug"]`` ONLY to shape
170	    ``out_root/<domain>/<slug>/``. We still reuse the gate0_lineage registry purely as a source
171	    of a descriptive name (task 72 -> ``drb_72_ai_labor``) so the run dir is legible and stable
172	    across runs; a task with no registered slug falls back to the bare ``drb_<id>``. Importing
173	    the registry constants does NOT read the (absent) DRB-II tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl вЂ” that
174	    read lived only inside run_gate_b_query's forced canonical binding, which we no longer hit.
175	    Import is lazy so this script's no-import-side-effect posture is unchanged.
176	    """
177	    import re  # noqa: PLC0415
178	
179	    from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage import (  # noqa: PLC0415
180	        DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD as _NO_GOLD,
181	        SLUG_TO_IDX as _SLUG_TO_IDX,
182	    )
183	
184	    for _slug in (*_SLUG_TO_IDX.keys(), *_NO_GOLD):
185	        _m = re.match(r"^drb_(\d+)_", _slug)
186	        if _m and _m.group(1) == str(tid):
187	            return _slug
188	    return f"drb_{tid}"

204	def _query_dict_for_task(task: dict) -> dict:
205	    """Build the ``q`` dict the fresh sweep entry (``run_one_query``) consumes.
206	
207	    run_one_query keys retrieval/generation/title on ``q["question"]`` вЂ” set here to the
208	    VERBATIM DRB-v1 task prompt (query.jsonl ``prompt``). ``slug``/``domain`` only shape the
209	    run dir. ``amplified`` is left empty so the gate's RetrievalProjection (threaded via
210	    PG_GATE=1 at the S2 hook, run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10435-10468) supplies the scope lanes вЂ”
211	    NOT a hand-written amplified list (that would bypass the no-starvation proof).
212	    """
213	    tid = str(task["id"])
214	    domain = _TASK_DOMAIN.get(tid, _DEFAULT_DOMAIN)
215	    # FAIL-LOUD at assembly: a domain outside SUPPORTED_DOMAINS makes run_scope_gate abort
216	    # BEFORE retrieval (manifest.status=abort_scope_rejected) вЂ” the exact live-probe failure
217	    # (domain='labor'). Catch it here at zero cost instead of after the live gate compile.
218	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.scope_gate import SUPPORTED_DOMAINS  # noqa: PLC0415
219	    if domain not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS:
220	        raise SystemExit(
221	            f"BLOCKED: task {tid} maps to domain {domain!r} which is NOT in scope_gate."
222	            f"SUPPORTED_DOMAINS ({sorted(SUPPORTED_DOMAINS)}); run_scope_gate would abort "
223	            f"pre-retrieval. Fix _TASK_DOMAIN in scripts/run_gate_e2e.py."
224	        )
225	    return {
226	        # Descriptive, stable run-dir slug (drb_72 -> drb_72_ai_labor). Cosmetic only now:
227	        # run_one_query does NO lineage lookup on the slug, so there is no forced official-
228	        # question rebind and no DRB-II gold-file read. q["question"] below is the sole prompt.
229	        "slug": _registered_slug_for_task(tid),
230	        # MUST be a scope_gate.SUPPORTED_DOMAINS member or run_scope_gate aborts pre-retrieval.
231	        "domain": domain,
232	        "question": task["prompt"],
233	        "amplified": [],
234	        "language": task.get("language", "en"),
235	    }

366	def _run_fresh_e2e(
367	    q: dict, out_root: Path, *, gate_on: bool, mode: str, runner: Any = None,
368	) -> tuple[Path, dict]:
369	    """EXECUTE the fresh e2e. Sets the gate env slate, calls run_one_query DIRECTLY, returns
370	    the run dir that holds report.md.
371	
372	    ``runner`` is the coroutine function invoked as ``runner(q, out_root)``. It defaults to the
373	    REAL ``scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query`` (the live path). The --dry-e2e mode and
374	    the offline integration test inject a NETWORK-MOCKED stub with the SAME (q, out_root) ->
375	    summary-dict interface, so the FULL harness path is exercised offline against run_one_query's
376	    real contract: same env slate, same q-dict, same report.md discovery, same abort detection.
377	
378	    WHY run_one_query (NOT run_gate_b_query): run_gate_b_query FORCES
379	    ``PG_BENCHMARK_OFFICIAL_QUESTION=1`` and rebinds ``q["question"]`` to the DeepResearch-
380	    Bench-II canonical gold question by reading ``third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/
381	    tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl`` via gate0_lineage вЂ” a file this repo does NOT ship (we score
382	    against DRB v1, task 72). That path fail-loud-raises GateZeroLineageError before any
383	    retrieval. ``run_one_query`` is the DIRECT home of the S2 gate hook
384	    (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10435-10468) and keys ALL of retrieval/generation/title on
385	    ``q["question"]`` вЂ” the VERBATIM DRB-v1 task-72 prompt supplied in ``q`` вЂ” with NO
386	    DRB-II coupling (the only gate0_lineage import inside run_one_query is guarded by
387	    ``resume=True``, which we never set). With ``PG_GATE=1`` + ``PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER=1``
388	    threaded by ``_fresh_e2e_env_slate`` below, run_one_query builds ``_research_plan`` and
389	    the S2 hook projects the RetrievalProjection into FS-Researcher (the no-starvation path);
390	    with PG_GATE=0 the hook stays byte-identical to champion. run_one_query writes
391	    ``report.md`` (+ manifest.json/protocol.json) to ``out_root/<domain>/<slug>/``.
392	
393	    Retrieval breadth (PG_SWEEP_* вЂ” 12/12/40) is applied by the real-run env, NOT here; this
394	    helper threads ONLY the gate switches so the byte-identical control (--no-gate) holds.
395	    """
396	    slate = _fresh_e2e_env_slate(gate_on=gate_on, mode=mode)
397	    for k, v in slate.items():
398	        os.environ[k] = v
399	    if runner is None:
400	        from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import run_one_query as runner
401	    # Capture run_one_query's summary вЂ” its ``status`` is the authoritative abort/success
402	    # signal. A scope reject (or any abort) returns status=abort_* AFTER writing a stub
403	    # report.md, so we can NOT infer success from report.md alone (that was the false-OK bug).
404	    summary = asyncio.run(runner(q, out_root))
405	    if not isinstance(summary, dict):
406	        summary = {"status": "error_no_summary", "error": "run_one_query returned non-dict"}
407	    return out_root / q["domain"] / q["slug"], summary
408	
409	
410	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
411	# Scoring commands (RACE + FACT) вЂ” documented; executed only when --score-* set on a live run
412	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
413	
414	def _race_command(report_path: Path, task_id: str) -> list[str]:
415	    return [
416	        sys.executable, "scripts/score_report_race.py",
417	        "--report", str(report_path),
418	        "--task-id", str(task_id),
419	        "--model-name", f"polaris_gate_task{task_id}",
420	    ]
421	
422	
423	def _fact_command(run_dir: Path, task_id: str) -> list[str]:
424	    """The FACT-utils path: build the claims ledger from the run's report + corpus, then score.
425	
426	    The billed span-fetcher + reconciled-audit judge are supplied by the operator-gated paid
427	    run (see scripts/dr_benchmark/score_run.py / build_claims_ledger.py). This returns the
428	    documented argv; the harness runs it only on a live --score-fact draw.
429	    """
430	    return [
431	        sys.executable, "-m", "scripts.dr_benchmark.build_claims_ledger",
432	        "--run-dir", str(run_dir),
433	        "--out-dir", str(run_dir / "fact_ledger"),
434	    ]
435	
436	
437	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
438	# Network-mocked run_one_query stub (the --dry-e2e / integration-test runner)
439	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
440	
441	def make_mock_run_one_query(
442	    *, expected_question: str, status: str = "released_with_disclosed_gaps",
443	    write_report: bool = True, assertions: bool = True,
444	):
445	    """Build a NETWORK-MOCKED stand-in for ``run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query``.
446	
447	    The returned coroutine has run_one_query's REAL interface вЂ” ``async (q, out_root) -> summary
448	    dict`` вЂ” but does ZERO network / LLM / retrieval / compose. It:
449	
450	      * ASSERTS (when ``assertions``) that the harness handed it an ACCEPTED scope-gate domain
451	        (in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS вЂ” the fix-(2) canary), the VERBATIM task question (byte-match vs
452	        ``expected_question`` вЂ” proves no prompt drift / no DRB-II rebind), and that the gate env
453	        slate threaded (PG_GATE + PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER present in os.environ вЂ” proves the S2
454	        projection would fire), replicating the exact preconditions the real run_one_query needs;
455	      * writes a realistic ``report.md`` to ``out_root/<domain>/<slug>/`` (the real run's output
456	        location) UNLESS ``write_report`` is False (used to prove the fail-loud "no report" path);
457	      * returns a summary dict shaped like run_one_query's real return (status/slug/domain/
458	        question/run_dir/error) with the requested terminal ``status`` вЂ” a non-abort status for
459	        the success case, or an ``abort_*`` status to prove fail-loud fires on the abort STUB.
460	    """
461	    async def _mock_run_one_query(q: dict, out_root: Path) -> dict:
462	        if assertions:
463	            from src.polaris_graph.nodes.scope_gate import SUPPORTED_DOMAINS
464	            assert q["domain"] in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS, (
465	                f"WIRING MISMATCH: run_one_query got domain {q['domain']!r} NOT in "
466	                f"SUPPORTED_DOMAINS вЂ” run_scope_gate would abort pre-retrieval."
467	            )
468	            assert q["question"] == expected_question, (
469	                "WIRING MISMATCH: run_one_query got a NON-VERBATIM question (prompt drift / "
470	                "unexpected rebind)."
471	            )
472	            assert os.environ.get("PG_GATE") in ("0", "1"), (
473	                "WIRING MISMATCH: PG_GATE not threaded into the run_one_query env."
474	            )
475	            assert "PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER" in os.environ, (
476	                "WIRING MISMATCH: PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER not threaded вЂ” the S2 projection "
477	                "would never fire (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10448 requires it)."
478	            )
479	        run_dir = out_root / q["domain"] / q["slug"]
480	        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
481	        if write_report:
482	            # A realistic non-trivial report the RACE/FACT stages can consume (sections + a
483	            # citation), NOT the abort stub. The bytes are synthetic; the WIRING is real.
484	            (run_dir / "report.md").write_text(
485	                f"# {q['question'][:60]}\n\n## Introduction\n\nMocked offline report body for "
486	                f"the dry-e2e wiring proof.\n\n## Findings\n\nFinding one [1].\n\n"
487	                f"## References\n\n[1] Example Source. (2025).\n",
488	                encoding="utf-8")
489	        return {
490	            "slug": q["slug"], "domain": q["domain"], "question": q["question"],
491	            "run_id": f"MOCK_{q['domain']}_{q['slug']}", "status": status,
492	            "run_dir": str(run_dir), "error": "" if not _is_abort_status(status) else status,
493	        }
494	    return _mock_run_one_query
495	
496	
497	def _run_cmd(cmd: list[str]) -> dict:
498	    t0 = time.time()
499	    try:
500	        proc = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=str(_REPO), capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=3600)
501	        return {"cmd": " ".join(cmd), "returncode": proc.returncode,
502	                "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t0, 1),
503	                "stdout_tail": proc.stdout[-800:], "stderr_tail": proc.stderr[-800:]}
504	    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
505	        return {"cmd": " ".join(cmd), "returncode": -1, "error": str(e)}
506	
507	
508	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
509	# Per-task orchestration
510	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
511	
512	def _process_task(
513	    task_id: str, *, out_root: Path, mode: str, gate_on: bool, dry: bool,
514	    draws: int, score_race: bool, score_fact: bool,
515	    dry_e2e: bool = False, e2e_runner: Any = None,
516	) -> dict:
517	    """Orchestrate one DRB task.
518	
519	    Three modes:
520	      * dry (default)   вЂ” gate+wiring assembly only, NO run_one_query call (spend-free smoke).
521	      * dry_e2e         вЂ” drive the FULL live harness path (gate offline stub + _run_fresh_e2e)
522	                          but with ``e2e_runner`` = a NETWORK-MOCKED run_one_query stub. Proves
523	                          the whole wiring offline; NO network, NO spend. The gate also runs on
524	                          the offline compiler stub (live=False) so no OPENROUTER call fires.
525	      * live            вЂ” the real fresh e2e (network + spend).
526	    """
527	    task = _load_drb_task(task_id)
528	    prompt = task["prompt"]
529	    q = _query_dict_for_task(task)
530	    # dry_e2e drives the live branch (executes run_one_query via the injected runner).
531	    take_live_branch = (not dry) or dry_e2e
532	    # The gate fires its real OpenRouter policy model ONLY on a true live run; dry AND dry_e2e
533	    # both use the offline compiler stub so the test/mode is fully spend-free.
534	    gate_live = (not dry) and (not dry_e2e)
535	    per_task: dict[str, Any] = {
536	        "task_id": task_id, "language": task.get("language"),
537	        "prompt_head": prompt[:70].replace("\n", " "),
538	        "mode": mode, "gate_on": gate_on, "dry": dry, "dry_e2e": dry_e2e, "draws": []}
539	
540	    for draw in range(1, draws + 1):
541	        run_id = f"gate_e2e_{task_id}_d{draw}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
542	        run_dir = out_root / q["domain"] / q["slug"] / f"draw{draw}"
543	        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
544	        d: dict[str, Any] = {"draw": draw, "run_dir": str(run_dir), "stages": {}}
545	
546	        # --- STAGE 1: GATE ---
547	        t0 = time.time()
548	        gate = _run_gate(prompt, mode=mode, live=gate_live, run_id=run_id)
549	        artifact = gate.artifact
550	        (run_dir / "planning_gate_artifact.json").write_text(
551	            json.dumps(artifact.to_dict(), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
552	        d["stages"]["gate"] = {
553	            "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t0, 2),
554	            "state": gate.state, "needs_input": gate.needs_input,
555	            "n_terms": len(artifact.contract.all_terms()),
556	            "n_hard": len(artifact.contract.hard_terms()),
557	            "n_assumptions": len(artifact.contract.assumptions),
558	            "n_coverage": len(artifact.contract.coverage),
559	            "contract_sha256": (getattr(artifact, "contract_sha256", "") or "")[:16],
560	            # the load-bearing autonomous invariant
561	            "autonomous_ok": (mode != "autonomous")
562	            or (gate.needs_input is False and gate.state in ("auto_pinned", "unsatisfiable")),
563	        }
564	
565	        # --- STAGE 2: retrieval / compose-render / audit WIRING projections ---
566	        d["stages"]["retrieval_wiring"] = _retrieval_wiring_proof(artifact, prompt)
567	        d["stages"]["compose_render_wiring"] = _compose_render_wiring_proof(artifact)
568	
569	        # --- STAGE 3: the assembled fresh-e2e pipeline call ---
570	        assembled = _assembled_pipeline_call(q, out_root, gate_on=gate_on, mode=mode)
571	        d["stages"]["fresh_e2e_call"] = assembled
572	
573	        if not take_live_branch:
574	            # Prove the audit wiring against a stub report (no compose). The REAL audit runs
575	            # on the fresh report.md in the live path below.
576	            stub_report = f"# {prompt[:50]}\n\n## Introduction\n\nStub.\n\n## References\n"
577	            audit_ok = True
578	            try:
579	                d["stages"]["audit"] = {
580	                    "on_stub_report": True,
581	                    "audit": _run_audit(
582	                        artifact, stub_report,
583	                        retrieval_scope_status="not_evaluated_dry_smoke"),
584	                }
585	            except Exception as _e:  # noqa: BLE001
586	                audit_ok = False
587	                d["stages"]["audit"] = {"on_stub_report": True, "error": str(_e)}
588	            # The DRY-SMOKE invariant is that the harness ASSEMBLES a complete, gate-fired
589	            # pipeline call вЂ” NOT that the deterministic offline stub emits rich queries (it
590	            # is deliberately minimal). We assert: (1) the gate pinned an artifact and the
591	            # autonomous invariant held; (2) all three projections compiled without error;
592	            # (3) the env slate + query dict assembled with PG_GATE set; (4) the audit wired.
593	            # NO-STARVATION at planning level: the gate candidate-query count can NEVER be
594	            # below the no-gate baseline (which adds 0 lanes) вЂ” gate_count >= 0 always holds,
595	            # and for the hard-scope canary (task 72) the journal/English constraint MUST
596	            # reach the projection as >=1 scope-anchored amplified query.
597	            rw = d["stages"]["retrieval_wiring"]
598	            no_starvation_ok = rw["candidate_query_count"] >= 0  # gate >= no_gate(=0)
599	            hard_scope_reached = (
600	                d["stages"]["gate"]["n_hard"] == 0  # no hard terms -> nothing to route
601	                or rw["amplified_query_count"] >= 1  # a hard term -> a scope-anchored lane
602	                or any(sz > 0 for sz in rw["scope_lane_sizes"].values()))
603	            d["stages"]["fresh_e2e_call"]["no_starvation_ok"] = no_starvation_ok
604	            d["stages"]["fresh_e2e_call"]["hard_scope_reached_projection"] = hard_scope_reached
605	            d["assembled_ok"] = bool(
606	                d["stages"]["gate"]["autonomous_ok"]
607	                and no_starvation_ok
608	                and hard_scope_reached
609	                and audit_ok
610	                and assembled["env_slate"]["PG_GATE"] in ("0", "1")
611	                and assembled["query_dict"]["question_head"])
612	        else:
613	            # --- LIVE: execute the fresh e2e, then audit + score the REAL report.md ---
614	            t1 = time.time()
615	            sweep_run_dir, sweep_summary = _run_fresh_e2e(
616	                q, out_root, gate_on=gate_on, mode=mode, runner=e2e_runner)
617	            report_path = sweep_run_dir / "report.md"
618	            sweep_status = sweep_summary.get("status")
619	            aborted = _is_abort_status(sweep_status)
620	            d["stages"]["fresh_e2e"] = {
621	                "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t1, 1),
622	                "sweep_run_dir": str(sweep_run_dir),
623	                "report_exists": report_path.exists(),
624	                "sweep_status": sweep_status,
625	                "sweep_error": sweep_summary.get("error") or "",
626	                "aborted": aborted,
627	            }
628	            # FAIL-LOUD: a run that ABORTED (scope reject, safety refusal, no-sources,
629	            # corpus-inadequate, budget, ...) or produced NO report.md is NOT a scoreable
630	            # report вЂ” even though the abort path writes a STUB report.md. Do NOT audit/score
631	            # a stub; set d["error"] so per-draw ok is False and main() exits non-zero.
632	            if aborted:
633	                d["error"] = (
634	                    f"fresh e2e ABORTED: status={sweep_status!r} "
635	                    f"{sweep_summary.get('error') or ''}".strip()
636	                )
637	            elif not report_path.exists():
638	                d["error"] = "fresh e2e produced no report.md (no abort status either)"
639	            else:
640	                report_text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
641	                # co-locate the judged report + artifact + audit in the harness run dir
642	                (run_dir / "report.md").write_text(report_text, encoding="utf-8")
643	                biblio_path = sweep_run_dir / "bibliography.json"
644	                audit = _run_audit(
645	                    artifact, report_text,
646	                    retrieval_scope_status="fresh_gate_scoped" if gate_on else "fresh_no_gate")
647	                (run_dir / "contract_compliance.json").write_text(
648	                    json.dumps(audit, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
649	                d["stages"]["audit"] = {"counts": audit.get("counts"),
650	                                        "retrieval_scope_status": audit.get("retrieval_scope_status")}
651	                if score_race:
652	                    d["stages"]["race"] = _run_cmd(_race_command(run_dir / "report.md", task_id))
653	                if score_fact:
654	                    d["stages"]["fact"] = _run_cmd(_fact_command(sweep_run_dir, task_id))
655	                _ = biblio_path  # referenced for provenance; audit already parses report refs
656	                d["live_ok"] = True
657	
658	        (run_dir / "gate_e2e_telemetry.json").write_text(
659	            json.dumps(d, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
660	        per_task["draws"].append(d)
661	
662	    return per_task
663	
664	
665	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
666	# CLI
667	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
668	
669	def main() -> int:
670	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="S5-HARNESS: fresh autonomous gate e2e + score.")
671	    ap.add_argument("--task-id", default=",".join(_S5_TASK_IDS),
672	                    help="comma-separated DRB task ids (default: the six S5 tasks).")
673	    ap.add_argument("--mode", default="autonomous", choices=("autonomous", "interactive"),
674	                    help="gate mode; the benchmark ALWAYS uses autonomous.")
675	    ap.add_argument("--draws", type=int, default=1, help="N draws per task (S5 protocol: 3).")
676	    ap.add_argument("--out-root", default="outputs/gate_e2e_s5", help="output root.")
677	    dry_grp = ap.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
678	    dry_grp.add_argument("--dry", action="store_true", default=True,
679	                         help="DRY SMOKE (default): gate+planning+wiring assembly, NO live "
680	                              "retrieval/compose. Spend-free, in-workflow safe.")
681	    dry_grp.add_argument("--plan-only", dest="dry", action="store_true",
682	                         help="alias for --dry.")
683	    dry_grp.add_argument("--live", dest="dry", action="store_false",
684	                         help="EXECUTE the real fresh e2e (>10 min, costs money). "
685	                              "Requires PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1 + OPENROUTER_API_KEY.")
686	    ap.add_argument("--dry-e2e", action="store_true", default=False,
687	                    help="OFFLINE full-path proof: drive the ENTIRE live harness path "
688	                         "(gate offline stub -> _run_fresh_e2e -> run_one_query) with the "
689	                         "network MOCKED (built-in stub). No spend, no OPENROUTER, in-workflow "
690	                         "safe. Proves the wiring the next LIVE run depends on.")
691	    ap.add_argument("--no-gate", dest="gate_on", action="store_false", default=True,
692	                    help="champion baseline: PG_GATE=0 (byte-identical control).")
693	    ap.add_argument("--score-race", action="store_true", help="run RACE on each live report.")
694	    ap.add_argument("--score-fact", action="store_true", help="run FACT on each live report.")
695	    args = ap.parse_args()
696	
697	    task_ids = [t.strip() for t in args.task_id.split(",") if t.strip()]
698	    out_root = (_REPO / args.out_root) if not Path(args.out_root).is_absolute() else Path(args.out_root)
699	    out_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
700	
701	    # --dry-e2e drives the LIVE branch with a mocked runner: NO real live run, so it needs
702	    # NEITHER OPENROUTER nor PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE. Only a true --live run is guarded.
703	    real_live = (not args.dry) and (not args.dry_e2e)
704	    # assembly_only = the default spend-free smoke (gate+wiring, no run_one_query at all).
705	    assembly_only = args.dry and (not args.dry_e2e)
706	    if real_live:
707	        if os.getenv("PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE", "0").strip().lower() not in ("1", "true", "yes", "on"):
708	            print("BLOCKED: --live requires PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1 (the gate's policy model).",
709	                  file=sys.stderr)
710	            return 2
711	        if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
712	            print("BLOCKED: --live requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY (source .env first).", file=sys.stderr)
713	            return 2
714	
715	    summary: dict[str, Any] = {
716	        "harness": "run_gate_e2e", "mode": args.mode, "dry": args.dry,
717	        "dry_e2e": args.dry_e2e,
718	        "gate_on": args.gate_on, "draws": args.draws, "out_root": str(out_root),
719	        "score_race": args.score_race, "score_fact": args.score_fact, "tasks": []}
720	
721	    all_ok = True
722	    _label = "DRY" if assembly_only else ("DRY-E2E(mocked)" if args.dry_e2e else "LIVE")
723	    for tid in task_ids:
724	        print(f"[gate-e2e] task {tid}  mode={args.mode}  "
725	              f"{_label}  gate={'ON' if args.gate_on else 'OFF'}  "
726	              f"draws={args.draws}")
727	        # In --dry-e2e, build a network-mocked run_one_query keyed on THIS task's verbatim
728	        # prompt so the stub asserts a byte-exact question match (no drift).
729	        _e2e_runner = None
730	        if args.dry_e2e:
731	            _task = _load_drb_task(tid)
732	            _e2e_runner = make_mock_run_one_query(expected_question=_task["prompt"])
733	        res = _process_task(
734	            tid, out_root=out_root, mode=args.mode, gate_on=args.gate_on,
735	            dry=assembly_only, dry_e2e=args.dry_e2e, e2e_runner=_e2e_runner,
736	            draws=args.draws,
737	            score_race=args.score_race, score_fact=args.score_fact)
738	        summary["tasks"].append(res)
739	        for d in res["draws"]:
740	            g = d["stages"]["gate"]
741	            rw = d["stages"]["retrieval_wiring"]
742	            # ASSEMBLY-ONLY: assembled_ok is authoritative. LIVE / DRY-E2E: the draw is OK only
743	            # if it produced a SCOREABLE report вЂ” d["live_ok"] is set True ONLY after a non-abort
744	            # report.md was audited (never on the abort stub). Any d["error"] (abort / missing
745	            # report) => NOT OK. This is the fix for the false "[gate-e2e] ALL OK" on an abort.
746	            if assembly_only:
747	                ok = bool(d.get("assembled_ok"))
748	            else:
749	                ok = bool(d.get("live_ok")) and not d.get("error")
750	            all_ok = all_ok and ok
751	            tail = f"assembled_ok={ok}" if assembly_only else (
752	                f"live_ok={ok} status="
753	                f"{d.get('stages', {}).get('fresh_e2e', {}).get('sweep_status')!r}"
754	                + (f" ERROR={d['error']}" if d.get("error") else ""))
755	            print(f"  draw{d['draw']}: gate={g['state']} "
756	                  f"terms={g['n_terms']} hard={g['n_hard']} cov={g['n_coverage']} "
757	                  f"amp_q={rw['amplified_query_count']} scope_lanes={len(rw['scope_lane_keys'])} "
758	                  f"{tail}")
759	
760	    summary["all_ok"] = all_ok
761	    summary_path = out_root / "gate_e2e_summary.json"
762	    summary_path.write_text(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
763	    print(f"[gate-e2e] summary -> {summary_path}")
764	    if not all_ok:
765	        # FAIL-LOUD: enumerate every draw that did NOT produce a scoreable report so a live
766	        # abort can NEVER be reported as success. This prints to stderr and returns non-zero.
767	        for res in summary["tasks"]:
768	            for d in res.get("draws", []):
769	                if d.get("error"):
770	                    print(f"[gate-e2e][FAIL] task {res['task_id']} draw{d['draw']}: "
771	                          f"{d['error']}", file=sys.stderr)
772	        print(f"[gate-e2e] FAIL ({len(task_ids)} tasks x {args.draws} draws, "
773	              f"{_label}) вЂ” at least one draw produced NO scoreable "
774	              f"report (abort / missing report.md). This run is NOT scoreable.",
775	              file=sys.stderr)
776	        return 1
777	    print(f"[gate-e2e] ALL OK "
778	          f"({len(task_ids)} tasks x {args.draws} draws, {_label})")
779	    return 0
780	
781	
782	if __name__ == "__main__":
783	    raise SystemExit(main())
784

+++ b/src/polaris_graph/planning/planning_gate_schema.py
@@ -1142,6 +1142,67 @@ def sha256_of(obj: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
     return hashlib.sha256(serialize_canonical(obj).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
 
 
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Span re-anchoring вЂ” the quote is authoritative, the offset is not
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+#
+# The contract compiler is an LLM. Empirically it copies the verbatim `quote`
+# for an explicit span correctly, but it CANNOT reliably count characters over a
+# multi-hundred-char prompt: its `start`/`end` drift by a few positions, so
+# `prompt[start:end] != quote` and the validator raises `span_quote_mismatch` on
+# otherwise-correct explicit terms (drb_72: 8 of 8 fatal span errors were pure
+# offset drift on quotes that appear verbatim in the prompt).
+#
+# This mirrors the S0 candidate adapter's own established discipline
+# (`candidate_adapter._locate_span`): the trigger PHRASE is the source of truth
+# and the offset is RE-DERIVED by locating that phrase verbatim in the prompt вЂ”
+# an offset is never trusted from an upstream that can't count. Re-anchoring is
+# a mechanical, no-invention operation: a span is corrected ONLY when its exact
+# `quote` still occurs verbatim in the prompt. A `quote` that is NOT present in
+# the prompt (a fabricated/paraphrased span) is left UNTOUCHED, so it stays a
+# fatal `span_quote_mismatch` and a hard term built on it is still rejected вЂ” the
+# no-invention rule (hard в‡’ origin==explicit в‡’ real verbatim span) is preserved.
+
+def _reanchor_span(span: PromptSpan, prompt: str) -> PromptSpan:
+    """Return a span whose offsets locate ``span.quote`` verbatim in ``prompt``.
+
+    If the span already matches (``prompt[start:end] == quote``) it is returned
+    unchanged. Otherwise, if ``quote`` occurs verbatim in the prompt (exact, then
+    case-insensitive with the ACTUAL prompt substring kept as the quote so
+    quote-equality holds), the offsets are corrected to that occurrence. If the
+    quote cannot be located at all, the span is returned unchanged (it will fail
+    validation вЂ” a fabricated span is never silently accepted).
+    """
+    if not prompt or not span.quote:
+        return span
+    if span.matches_prompt(prompt):
+        return span
+    idx = prompt.find(span.quote)
+    if idx != -1:
+        return PromptSpan(idx, idx + len(span.quote), span.quote)
+    low = prompt.lower().find(span.quote.lower())
+    if low != -1:
+        actual = prompt[low:low + len(span.quote)]
+        return PromptSpan(low, low + len(actual), actual)
+    return span
+
+
+def reanchor_contract_spans(contract: ResearchContract, prompt: str) -> ResearchContract:
+    """Re-derive every term's span offsets from its verbatim quote (in place).
+
+    Called by the compiler after parsing and BEFORE validation so the LLM's
+    unreliable character offsets never trip ``span_quote_mismatch`` on a quote
+    that is genuinely present in the prompt. A quote absent from the prompt is
+    left as-is and still fails validation вЂ” this only corrects arithmetic, it
+    never invents support for a term. Returns the same contract for chaining.
+    """
+    prompt = prompt or ""
+    for term in contract.all_terms():
+        if term.spans:
+            term.spans = [_reanchor_span(sp, prompt) for sp in term.spans]
+    return contract
+
+

 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

+        # The LLM copies the verbatim quote correctly but drifts on character
+        # offsets; re-derive each span from its quote (no-invention: a quote not
+        # present in the prompt is left to fail validation). Mirrors the S0
+        # candidate adapter's _locate_span discipline.
+        reanchor_contract_spans(contract, prompt)
         errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
         if not _fatal_errors(errors, mode=mode):

@@ -714,6 +720,7 @@ async def _compile_contract(

         data2 = _loads(raw2)

content.text: This is the core fix (2): the compiler was hitting `span_quote_mismatch` fatal errors because the LLM drifts on character offsets. The `reanchor_contract_spans` re-derives offsets from the verbatim quote before validation. The no-invention rule is preserved (a quote not in the prompt stays failing). Let me look at the tests.
